# Scaffold Chubb APAC Policy Management Platform — Full Solution

Scaffold the complete solution for the **Chubb APAC Policy Management Platform** assessment.
Use C# / .NET 8 for the backend and Angular 17+ for the frontend.
Follow Clean Architecture with strict layer boundaries — dependencies must point inward only.

---

## Step 1 — Create the .NET Solution

```
src/
  Chubb.PolicyManagement.sln
```

Create the solution file at `src/Chubb.PolicyManagement.sln` and add all projects below to it.

---

## Step 2 — Create Backend Projects (Clean Architecture)

Scaffold the following .NET projects **in this order**, each in its own folder under `src/backend/`:

### 2.1 Domain Layer — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Domain`
- Class library (no framework dependencies)
- Contains: entities, value objects, enums, domain exceptions, repository interfaces
- Key types to create:
  - `Policy` entity with all 14 fields from the schema (id, policyNumber, policyholderName, lineOfBusiness, status, premiumAmount, currency, effectiveDate, expiryDate, region, underwriter, flaggedForReview, createdAt, updatedAt)
  - `LineOfBusiness` enum: `Property`, `Casualty`, `AAndH`, `Marine`
  - `PolicyStatus` enum: `Active`, `Expired`, `Pending`, `Cancelled`
  - `IPolicyRepository` interface with: `GetPagedAsync`, `GetByIdAsync`, `BulkFlagAsync`, `GetSummaryAsync`
  - `PolicyNotFoundException` domain exception
- **No references to EF Core, ASP.NET, or any infrastructure package**

### 2.2 Application Layer — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Application`
- Class library
- References: `Domain` only
- Contains: use case services, DTOs, query/command models, pagination models, mapping interfaces
- Key types to create:
  - `PolicyService` (implements `IPolicyService`) with methods: `GetPoliciesAsync`, `GetPolicyByIdAsync`, `BulkFlagPoliciesAsync`, `GetSummaryAsync`
  - `IPolicyService` interface
  - `PolicyDto`, `PolicySummaryDto`, `PagedResult<T>`, `PolicyFilterQuery` (with page, size, sort, status, lineOfBusiness, region, effectiveDateFrom, effectiveDateTo, search fields)
  - `BulkFlagRequest` DTO
- **No references to EF Core, ASP.NET, or infrastructure**

### 2.3 Infrastructure Layer — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure`
- Class library
- References: `Domain`, `Application`
- Contains: EF Core DbContext, entity configurations, repository implementations, migrations folder
- Key types to create:
  - `PolicyManagementDbContext` with `DbSet<Policy>`
  - `PolicyEntityConfiguration` (IEntityTypeConfiguration)
  - `PolicyRepository` implementing `IPolicyRepository`
  - `InfrastructureServiceExtensions` for DI registration
- Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore`, `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer`, `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design`

### 2.4 API Layer — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api`
- ASP.NET Core Web API (.NET 8)
- References: `Application`, `Infrastructure`
- Contains: controllers, middleware, problem details handler, health checks, OpenAPI config
- Key types to create:
  - `PoliciesController` with actions for all 4 endpoints:
    - `GET /api/v1/policies` — paginated, filtered, sorted list
    - `GET /api/v1/policies/{id}` — single policy
    - `PATCH /api/v1/policies/flag` — bulk flag
    - `GET /api/v1/policies/summary` — aggregated stats
  - Global exception middleware returning RFC 7807 problem details
  - Health check endpoint at `/health`
  - `Program.cs` with full DI wiring, Swagger/OpenAPI, CORS, structured logging (Serilog)
- Add NuGet packages: `Swashbuckle.AspNetCore`, `Serilog.AspNetCore`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Diagnostics.HealthChecks`

---

## Step 3 — Create Test Projects

### 3.1 Unit Tests — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Tests.Unit`
- xUnit project under `src/backend/tests/`
- References: `Application`, `Domain`
- Scaffold test classes (empty, ready to implement):
  - `PolicyServiceTests` — unit tests for service layer using mocked repository
  - `PolicyEntityTests` — domain entity validation tests
- Add NuGet: `xunit`, `Moq`, `FluentAssertions`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`

### 3.2 Integration Tests — `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Tests.Integration`
- xUnit project under `src/backend/tests/`
- References: `Api`, `Infrastructure`
- Scaffold test classes:
  - `PoliciesControllerIntegrationTests` — uses `WebApplicationFactory<Program>`
  - `PolicyRepositoryIntegrationTests` — uses in-memory or test SQL Server
- Add NuGet: `xunit`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`, `FluentAssertions`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`

---

## Step 4 — Create Angular Frontend Project

Scaffold under `src/frontend/`:

```
src/frontend/
  policy-dashboard/          ← Angular 17+ app (standalone components)
    src/
      app/
        core/                ← services, interceptors, guards
        features/
          policies/          ← policy list, detail, flag components
          summary/           ← summary statistics panel component
        shared/              ← reusable UI components, pipes, directives
      environments/
    angular.json
    package.json
    tsconfig.json
```

- Use **Angular 17+ standalone components** (no NgModules)
- Use **Angular Signals** or NgRx for state management
- Configure `HttpClient` to point to the backend API
- Add `angular.json` with production and development build configurations
- Include a `proxy.conf.json` to proxy `/api` to `http://localhost:5000` for local dev

---

## Step 5 — Create Docker Compose and Config Files

### `docker-compose.yml` at workspace root
Orchestrate three services:
```yaml
services:
  db:          # SQL Server 2022
  api:         # Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api
  frontend:    # Angular app (nginx)
```
- `db`: use `mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest`, expose port 1433, set SA_PASSWORD from env
- `api`: build from `src/backend/Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api`, port 5000, depends on `db`, set `ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection` from env
- `frontend`: build from `src/frontend/policy-dashboard`, port 4200, depends on `api`

### `docker-compose.override.yml`
Local development overrides with sensible default env values (SA password, connection string).

### `.env.example`
Document all required environment variables:
```
MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD=
ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection=
ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Development
```

### `src/backend/Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api/appsettings.json`
```json
{
  "ConnectionStrings": { "DefaultConnection": "" },
  "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Default": "Information" } },
  "AllowedHosts": "*"
}
```

### `src/backend/Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api/appsettings.Development.json`
Development overrides with localhost SQL Server connection string placeholder.

---

## Step 6 — Create OpenAPI Specification File

Create `docs/openapi.yaml` at the workspace root containing a complete OpenAPI 3.x specification for all 4 endpoints:

- `GET /api/v1/policies` — with all query parameters (page, size, sort, status, lineOfBusiness, region, effectiveDateFrom, effectiveDateTo, search)
- `GET /api/v1/policies/{id}`
- `PATCH /api/v1/policies/flag` — request body: `{ "policyIds": ["uuid"] }`
- `GET /api/v1/policies/summary`

Include full schema definitions for `Policy`, `PolicySummary`, `PagedPolicyResponse`, `BulkFlagRequest`, and RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails`.

---

## Step 7 — Create README

Create `README.md` at workspace root with:
- Prerequisites (Docker Desktop, .NET 8 SDK, Node 20+, Angular CLI)
- Quick start: `docker-compose up --build`
- How to run backend only: `dotnet run` from API project
- How to run frontend only: `ng serve`
- How to run tests: `dotnet test` and `ng test`
- Environment variable reference table
- Project structure diagram

---

## Expected Final Structure

```
/
├── README.md
├── docker-compose.yml
├── docker-compose.override.yml
├── .env.example
├── docs/
│   └── openapi.yaml
└── src/
    ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.sln
    ├── backend/
    │   ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Domain/
    │   ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Application/
    │   ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure/
    │   ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api/
    │   └── tests/
    │       ├── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Tests.Unit/
    │       └── Chubb.PolicyManagement.Tests.Integration/
    └── frontend/
        └── policy-dashboard/
```

---

## Constraints

- All connection strings and secrets via environment variables — never hardcoded
- No circular project references — enforce dependency direction: Api → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
- Each project must build independently (`dotnet build` on each)
- Solution must build clean with `dotnet build src/Chubb.PolicyManagement.sln`
- Use `nullable` enabled and `implicit usings` in all .NET projects
- Target `net8.0` for all backend projects
